content: Compound interest is a powerful force that can help your savings grow over time. It's not a get-rich-quick scheme, but a simple concept that can make a big difference in the long run. Here's how it works: when you earn interest on your savings, that interest is added to your principal balance. Then, in the next period, you earn interest on both the original amount and the interest that was added. This creates a snowball effect, where your savings grow faster and faster over time. For example, if you save $1,000 per year for 10 years and earn 5% interest, you'll have around $13,000 after 10 years. But if you continue to save and earn interest for another 10 years, your total will be over $30,000. By harnessing the power of compound interest, you can build wealth steadily and securely.
